executive_summary: Canada is experiencing a severe ransomware crisis with 105 Canadian organizations breached in the last 48 hours across all sectors. Major incidents include Canada Goose (coinbasecartel), National Money Mart with database leaked (everest), Eastern Townships School Board (qilin), and Peterborough Public Health (lynx). Additionally, the Canadian Centre for Cyber Security issued two critical alerts: CVE-2026-31431 affecting Linux systems and CVE-2026-41940 affecting cPanel/WHM hosting platforms. The Qilin ransomware group is the most active threat actor with 18 Canadian victims, followed by Play (10 victims) and Clop (13 victims).

items.description: Vulnerability affecting cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM)

items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1589 (Gather Victim Identity Information)

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces an unprecedented ransomware crisis with 105 organizations breached in the last 48 hours across all sectors and provinces. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) has issued two high-priority alerts: CVE-2026-31431 affecting Linux systems and CVE-2026-41940 affecting cPanel/WHM web hosting platforms. Critical infrastructure targets include public health agencies, municipalities, school boards, telecommunications, and essential services.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (major apparel retailer) breached by CoinbaseCartel ransomware gang; Active Green + Ross (automotive retail chain) breached by Sinobi
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (financial services) breached by Everest ransomware with database leaked; Morison Insurance Brokers breached by Sinobi; Trucash (financial services) breached by Lynx

report_2w.executive_summary: Cisco ASA and Firepower devices face critical vulnerabilities (CVE-2025-20333, CVE-2025-20362, CVE-2025-20363) actively exploited to deploy the FIRESTARTER backdoor, which survives patches and requires full device cold starts to remove. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security has issued an alert on these flaws. Separately, threat actors ShinyHunters claim breaches of Medtronic, Vimeo, and Udemy affecting millions of records, while Silk Typhoon member was extradited to the U.S. for COVID research cyberattacks. North Korean Lazarus group (BlueNoroff) is targeting cryptocurrency firms with AI-generated Zoom meeting lures and ClickFix techniques.

items.description: Cisco ASA and FTD vulnerability enabling FIRESTARTER backdoor deployment
items.software_affected: Cisco ASA and Firepower Threat Defense (FTD) devices
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: Medium, Availability: Low

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Medtronic (medical device manufacturer), Vimeo Inc., Udemy (1.4M records), claiming multiple data breaches
items.ttps: T1589.002 (Gather Victim Identity Information), T1567.002 (Exfiltration to Cloud Storage), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), extortion via dark web leak sites

items.name: Silk Typhoon (Chinese state-sponsored APT)
items.targets: COVID research institutions in the U.S.
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1189 (Drive-by Compromise), T1071 (Application Layer Protocol), espionage targeting health research

items.name: Lazarus Group / BlueNoroff (North Korean APT)
items.targets: Cryptocurrency firms and developers
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Spearphishing Attachment), T1204.002 (Malicious File), T1027 (Obfuscated Files), ClickFix social engineering, AI-generated Zoom meeting lures, PromptMink malware, software supply chain attacks via npm

items.name: Sandworm (Russian state-sponsored APT)
items.targets: Multiple sectors for long-term espionage
items.ttps: T1572 (Protocol Tunneling), T1090.003 (Proxy: Multi-hop), SSH-over-Tor tunnels for stealthy persistence

items.name: OilRig (Iranian APT, APT34)
items.targets: Middle Eastern organizations
items.ttps: T1001.002 (Data Obfuscation: Steganography), T1071.001 (Web Protocols), C2 configuration hidden in Google Drive images via LSB steganography

items.name: PhantomCore
items.targets: Russian networks via TrueConf vulnerabilities
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1566.001 (Spearphishing), ClickFix techniques

items.name: Scattered Spider (UNC3944)
items.targets: Multiple U.S. organizations
items.ttps: T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1566.002 (Spearphishing Link), T1528 (Steal Application Access Token), social engineering for initial access

items.name: RansomHouse
items.targets: Barracuda Networks (unconfirmed claim), cybersecurity vendor
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), double extortion

items.name: Qilin (ransomware group)

items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1567.002 (Exfiltration to Cloud Storage), double extortion ransomware

items.targets: ANZ organizations, retail sector
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1567.002 (Exfiltration to Cloud Storage)

items.name: DragonForce (ransomware group)

items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567.002 (Exfiltration to Cloud Storage)

items.name: Chinese-backed smishing rings

items.ttps: T1566.003 (Phishing: Spearphishing via Service), T1056.003 (Input Capture: Web Portal Capture), SMS and OTT app-based credential phishing

items.ttps: T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise: Software Supply Chain), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL25-012 on April 23, 2026, warning of critical vulnerabilities in Cisco ASA and FTD devices (CVE-2025-20333, CVE-2025-20362, CVE-2025-20363) being actively exploited to deploy the FIRESTARTER backdoor. Manitoba announced plans to ban social media and AI chatbots for youth. No specific breaches of major Canadian organizations were identified in this period.
